skill.name: meta-cognitive-reasoning
skill.description: Provides disciplined reasoning frameworks for evidence-based analysis, hypothesis testing, temporal verification, and self-correction during reviews, debugging, and decisions.
skill.summary: Use structured reasoning to verify claims before drawing conclusions.
skill.author: Joseph OBrien

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, reconnaissance, and weak-crypto patterns. Review found they are Markdown examples and reasoning text, not executable code. No prompt injection, exfiltration, scripts, or runtime hooks were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Command Examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: false positive. Fenced Markdown examples show evidence-gathering commands, but the skill has no executable wrapper or command invocation code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All scanned files are Markdown guidance. The command-like strings appear in examples and prose, with no scripts or runtime entry points.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hardcoded URL Examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: false positive. The URLs appear inside package-version examples; there is no network client or request code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited URLs are sample registry metadata in documentation. No HTTP call, webhook, credential handling, or exfiltration path was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Terms
low_findings.description: Verdict: false positive. Detected words occur in prose, example hashes, or documentation-quality examples, not security-sensitive code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are natural-language reasoning guidance or examples. No cryptographic API use, scanner logic, or reconnaissance automation was present.

content.value_statement: Fast analysis often leads to unsupported claims and premature decisions. This skill guides Claude and Codex through evidence, hypotheses, verification, and correction.
actual_capabilities: Structures analysis around observations, mechanisms, and assessments.
Prompts verification before claims about versions, files, tests, or systems.
Generates competing hypotheses before recommending fixes.
Supports code review, debugging, architecture review, and documentation analysis.
Provides self-correction steps when earlier reasoning was wrong.
Helps prioritize findings with evidence and clear completion criteria.
limitations: It does not run tools by itself or verify facts without agent action.
It does not replace domain expertise for specialized decisions.
It may add process overhead for very simple tasks.
It depends on the user or agent providing accurate context.

use_cases.title: Evidence-Based Code Review
use_cases.description: Review changes with file evidence, competing explanations, and calibrated severity.

use_cases.title: Complex Debugging Triage
use_cases.description: Separate symptoms from causes before choosing a fix path.

use_cases.title: Decision Quality Checks
use_cases.description: Test assumptions, verify time-sensitive facts, and document reasoning before decisions.

prompt_templates.title: Verify One Claim
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner check for a single uncertain statement.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use meta-cognitive reasoning to verify this claim before accepting it: [claim]. Show evidence first, then interpretation.

prompt_templates.title: Review With Evidence
prompt_templates.scenario: Structured review of code, documentation, or a plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review [artifact] using evidence-based reasoning. For each finding, show the evidence, mechanism, impact, and recommended action.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Competing Hypotheses
prompt_templates.scenario: Debugging when one symptom has several possible causes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this issue with multiple working hypotheses: [issue]. Identify discriminating evidence before recommending a fix.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Reasoning Failure Risk
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of a prior analysis or high-impact decision.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this analysis for unsupported claims, stale knowledge, skipped mechanisms, and premature conclusions: [analysis]. Provide corrections with evidence.

output_examples.input: A review says a package version cannot exist.
output: Observation: The version looks newer than expected.
Hypotheses: It is invalid, it is newly released, or the lockfile is stale.
Verification: Check the lockfile and authoritative registry before recommending changes.
Assessment: Do not mark it as a blocker until evidence confirms invalidity.

output_examples.input: Several files appear to have identical content.
output: Observation: The files match by content.
Hypotheses: They are copies, symlinks, generated outputs, or intentional entry points.
Verification: Inspect file metadata and generation steps.
Assessment: The mechanism determines whether consolidation is useful.

output_examples.input: Five tests fail with timeout errors.
output: Observation: Multiple tests show similar timeout symptoms.
Hypotheses: One shared dependency failed, setup is broken, or each test has a separate issue.
Verification: Compare timing, dependencies, and isolated runs.
Assessment: Fix the proven cause, not the first familiar pattern.

best_practices: Show evidence before interpretation for every important claim.
Generate at least two plausible hypotheses before diagnosing complex issues.
Verify time-sensitive facts with current sources before making recommendations.
anti_patterns: Marking issues as critical without direct evidence.
Assuming unknown versions or features do not exist.
Applying a familiar fix before identifying the actual mechanism.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code reason from evidence during reviews, debugging, and decisions.

faq.question: Does it execute commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides reasoning frameworks and examples. The agent must choose and run any needed tools.

faq.question: Is it only for code review?
faq.answer: No. It also supports debugging, architecture review, documentation review, and decision analysis.

faq.question: How does it prevent bad conclusions?
faq.answer: It asks for evidence, competing hypotheses, discriminating checks, and calibrated assessments before recommendations.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it when a task has uncertainty, high impact, many possible causes, or time-sensitive facts.

faq.question: What should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the artifact, claim, issue, or decision context, plus any constraints or success criteria.
